data.body: PASS 1 review (G6 — approach soundness). Affirmations: S1 cache mechanism verified correct & fit-for-purpose against code (parent plan lives in system_prompt at passes.py:257; anthropic-gated, provider-safe runner seam at runner.py:421-435; no RunRequest content-list change preserves the structured-output retry path) — positive rationale stated throughout. S3's 'don't shed G3/G4 + fix cost model' is grounded (shed_to_budget sheds container at sizing.py:221-226; project() multiplies container by n_children at line 206) and the strict S3->S4->S5 sequencing note is well-reasoned. Approach selection (thread-pool + warm-then-fan-out, eval-gated batching) is defensible. One coaching finding: the plan adds 'a NEW pool inside _run_container' but does not specify how the parallel fan-out aggregates results safely — _run_container currently mutates a shared `out` via out.extend() and reads len(out) for per-pairing counts (pass1.py:104,106,125,142); the concurrent aggregation path should be specified (futures->ordered collect, not shared-list mutation).
